get.summary: Retrieve all accessible collection records.
get.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

post.summary: Create a new collection.
post.description:
Only Curators can use this API endpoint. 
The authenticated API user must have the "Create Collections" permission to use this endpoint, else 401 Unauthorized will be returned.

200.description: The id of the newly created collection

get.summary: Retrieve details about an existing collection.
get.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing collection.

put.summary: Update the name and/or owner of an existing collection.
put.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing collection.

delete.summary: Delete an existing collection.
delete.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing collection.

put.summary: Update a user's permissions within an existing collection.
put.description:
Only Curators can use this API endpoint.
For Windows Authentication configured Server instances, provide the Active Directory Sid for the userId parameter.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing collection.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description:
Identifier for an existing user.
For Windows Authentication-configured Server instances, the Active Directory sid should be provided instead.

put.summary: Update a user group's permissions within an existing collection.
put.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing collection.

parameters.name: userGroupId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user group.

post.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing collection.

parameters.name: addUsersContract
parameters.description: User and permissions information.

post.summary: Add a schedule to an existing collection.
post.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing collection.

post.summary: Add a workflow to an existing collection.
post.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing collection.

post.summary: Add a user group to an existing collection.
post.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing collection.

parameters.name: addUserGroupsContract
parameters.description: User Group and permissions information.

delete.summary: Remove a user from an existing collection.
delete.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing collection.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user.

delete.summary: Remove a schedule from an existing collection.

parameters.description: Identifier for an existing collection.

parameters.name: scheduleId

delete.summary: Remove a workflow from an existing collection.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing collection.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing workflow or app.

delete.summary: Remove a user group from an existing collection.
delete.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: collectionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing collection.

parameters.name: userGroupId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user group.

get.summary: Retrieve details about an existing credential.

parameters.name: credentialId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing credential.

put.description: This endpoint cannot be used unless Server is configured with SSL/TLS enabled.

parameters.name: credentialId
parameters.description: The ID of the credential to update.

delete.summary: Delete an existing credential.
delete.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: credentialId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing credential.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: cleans up any shares

get.summary: Retrieve all accessible credential records.
get.description: If you are not a Curator, then results will be limited to credentials that you have access to and the following arguments will be disregarded: view, userId, and userGroupId.

parameters.name: view
parameters.description: Determines the amount of information about each credential being returned.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Filter results for a specific user.

parameters.name: userGroupId
parameters.description: Filter results for a specific user group.

post.summary: Add a credential.
post.description: This endpoint cannot be used unless Server is configured with SSL/TLS enabled.

post.summary: Share an existing credential with an existing user.
post.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: credentialId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing credential.

post.summary: Share an existing credential with an existing user group.
post.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: credentialId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing credential.

delete.summary: Remove a user's permissions to use an existing credential.
delete.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: credentialId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing credential.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user.

delete.summary: Remove a user group's permission to use an existing credential.
delete.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: credentialId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing credential.

parameters.name: userGroupId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user group.

get.summary: Retrieves details for a specific DCM Connection record by its ID.

parameters.name: Id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the DCM Connection.

200.description: Returns the Connection with the specified ID.

404.description: The specified Connection does not exist or access is denied.

delete.summary: Deletes a DCM Connection by its ID.

parameters.name: Id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the DCM Connection.

200.description: Returns details of the deleted Connection.

404.description: The specified Connection does not exist or access is denied.

get.summary: Retrieves a DCM Connection referenced in workflows.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the DCM Connection.

200.description: Returns the Connection for the specified connectionId.

400.description: The connectionId is missing or invalid.

404.description: The specified Connection does not exist or access is denied.

put.summary: Shares a DCM Connection with specific users and groups for execution.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the DCM Connection.

parameters.name: sharingContract
parameters.description: Specifies the credentials, users, and groups (by ID) for sharing the DCM Connection for execution.

400.description: The request payload was invalid or malformed.

401.description: The user is not authorized or is not allowed to share Connections

404.description: The specified Connection does not exist or access is denied.

delete.summary: Removes execution sharing from a DCM Connection.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the shared DCM Connection.

400.description: The request payload was invalid or malformed.

401.description: The user is not authorized or is not allowed to share Connections

404.description: The specified Connection does not exist or access is denied.

put.summary: Shares a DCM Connection with specific users and groups for collaboration.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the DCM Connection.

parameters.name: sharingContract
parameters.description: Specifies the credentials, users, and groups (by ID) for sharing the DCM Connection for collaboration.

200.description: Returns the collaboration sharing details for the Connection.

400.description: The request payload was invalid or malformed.

401.description: The user is not authorized or is not allowed to share Connections

404.description: The specified Connection does not exist or access is denied.

post.summary: Creates a new DCM Connection or updates an existing one.

parameters.name: upsertConnectionContract
parameters.description: Specify Connection details for creation or update.

400.description: The request payload was invalid or malformed.

404.description: The specified Connection does not exist or access is denied.

get.summary: Retrieves details for a specific DCM Connection record by its ID.
get.description:
### Only Curators can use this API endpoint.
---

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the DCM Connection.

200.description: Returns the Connection with the specified ID.

400.description: The request was invalid or an error occurred.

401.description: The user is not authorized or does not have the 'Curator' role.

404.description: The specified Connection does not exist.

delete.summary: Deletes a DCM Connection by its ID.
delete.description:
### Only Curators can use this API endpoint.
---

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the DCM Connection.

200.description: Returns details of the deleted Connection.

400.description: The request was invalid or an error occurred.

401.description: The user is not authorized or does not have the 'Curator' role.

404.description: The specified Connection does not exist or access is denied.

get.summary: Retrieves the complete list of DCME ConnectionHandlingRules.
get.description:
### Only Curators can use this API endpoint.
---

200.description: Returns the list of all ConnectionHandlingRules.

400.description: The request was invalid or an error occurred.

401.description: The user is not authorized or does not have the 'Curator' role.

post.summary: Creates a new DCME ConnectionHandlingRule if the ID is null, or updates an existing one if the ID is provided.
post.description:
**Only Curators can use this API endpoint.**
---
            
### Example Use Case
*Create* a new rule:
            
```json
{
    "sourceConnectionId": "11111111-1111-1111-1111-111111111111",
    "sourceConnectionTitle": "Legacy Connection",
    "targetConnectionId": "22222222-2222-2222-2222-222222222222",
    "targetConnectionTitle": "New Standardized Connection"
}
```

parameters.name: handlingRule
parameters.description: The DCME contract used to create or update a ConnectionHandlingRule.

400.description: The request payload was invalid or malformed.

401.description: The user is not authorized or does not have the 'Curator' role.

404.description: The specified ConnectionHandlingRule does not exist or access is denied.

get.summary: Retrieves a DCME ConnectionHandlingRule by its unique ID.
get.description:
### Only Curators can use this API endpoint.
---

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the DCME ConnectionHandlingRule.

400.description: The request was invalid or an error occurred.

401.description: The user is not authorized or does not have the 'Curator' role.

404.description: The specified ConnectionHandlingRule does not exist.

delete.summary: Deletes a DCME ConnectionHandlingRule by its unique ID.
delete.description:
### Only Curators can use this API endpoint.
---

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the DCME ConnectionHandlingRule to delete.

400.description: The request was invalid or an error occurred.

401.description: The user is not authorized or does not have the 'Curator' role.

404.description: The specified ConnectionHandlingRule does not exist.

get.summary: Retrieves a list of DCM Connection records.
get.description:
### Only Curators can use this API endpoint.
---

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: Filters Connections by a specific ConnectionId.

parameters.name: visibleBy
parameters.description: When provided, returns the same results that the specified user (by *userId*) would see.

200.description: Returns the list of all Connections in the database.

400.description: The request was invalid or an error occurred.

401.description: The user is not authorized or does not have the 'Curator' role.

post.summary: Creates a new DCM Connection or updates an existing one on behalf of a user.

parameters.name: upsertConnectionContract
parameters.description: Specify Connection details for creation or update.

400.description: The request payload was invalid or malformed.

401.description: The user is not authorized or does not have the 'Curator' role.

404.description: The specified Connection does not exist or access is denied.

delete.summary: Removes execution sharing from a DCM Connection.
delete.description:
### Only Curators can use this API endpoint.
---

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the shared DCM Connection.

400.description: The request was invalid or an error occurred.

401.description: The user is not authorized or does not have the 'Curator' role.

delete.summary: Removes collaboration sharing from a DCM Connection.
delete.description:
### Only Curators can use this API endpoint.
---

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the shared DCM Connection.

400.description: The request was invalid or an error occurred.

401.description: The user is not authorized or does not have the 'Curator' role.

get.summary: Retrieve details about an existing DCM connection.
get.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: connectionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing DCM connection.

get.summary: Retrieve all messages of a specific job.
get.description: Only Curators can use this endpoint.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: Identifier for a job that has been run.

get.summary: Retrieve details about an existing job and its current state.
get.description: Only app workflows can be used.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: Identifier for a job that's running or in queue.

delete.summary: Cancel the running job or delete a queued job.
delete.description: Curators can cancel any job, while other roles can cancel jobs they have created, triggered, or scheduled.

parameters.name: jobId
parameters.description: Identifier for a job that is to be cancelled.

get.summary: Get status of queued jobs.
get.description: Curators can view the jobs in the queue.

parameters.name: sortField
parameters.description: Field to sort by: Values may be CreateDate, AppName, CompletionDateTime, Priority, Status

parameters.name: direction
parameters.description: Sort direction: Values may be asc,desc

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Offset for paging.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Limit for paging.

parameters.name: priorities
parameters.description: Job priorities filter: Values may be Low, Medium, High, Critical and Numeric Values

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description: Job statuses filter: Values may be: Queued, Initializing, Running, Complete

parameters.name: users
parameters.description: User filter: Valid userId (Filters only if allUsers = true)

parameters.name: allUsers
parameters.description: Include all users?

parameters.name: workflowNames
parameters.description: Workflow names filter: Accepts WorkflowName

parameters.name: completedBefore
parameters.description: Completed before [date]. Date format yyyy-MM-dd

parameters.name: completedAfter
parameters.description: Completed on or after [date].Date format yyyy-MM-dd

get.summary: Retrieve details about an existing schedule.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing schedule.

put.summary: Update details of an existing schedule.
put.description:
If TimeZone is empty or skipped then all the datetime fields will be according to timezone UTC-0 
and if the TimeZone is given then all the datetime fields will be according to that timezone provided.
Click https://help.alteryx.com/current/en/server/api-overview/alteryx-server-api-v3/server-api-v3-objects/schedules-endpoints.html#list-of-timezones in order to view the list of valid timezones.
Click https://help.alteryx.com/current/en/server/api-overview/alteryx-server-api-v3/server-api-v3-objects/schedules-endpoints.html#update-an-existing-schedule in order to view Help Documentation for this endpoint.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing schedule.

delete.summary: Delete an existing schedule.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing schedule.

patch.summary: Update details of an existing schedule.
patch.description: Click Alteryx List of Time Zones in order to view the list of valid timezones.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing schedule.

get.summary: Retrieve all accessible schedule records.
get.description: When filtering using RunsAfter or RunsBefore, both parameters must be used in tandem. You are limited to 45 days apart from each other.

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description: Filter results for a specific owner.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: Filter results for a specific workflow.

parameters.name: runsAfter
parameters.description: Filters by whether a schedule will run after a given date, inclusive. Should be provided as UTC-0.

parameters.name: runsBefore
parameters.description: Filters by whether a schedule will run after a given date, exclusive. Should be provided as UTC-0.

parameters.name: view
parameters.description: How much information about each schedule to return.

post.summary: Create a new schedule.
post.description:
If TimeZone is empty or skipped then all the datetime fields will be according to timezone UTC-0 and 
if the TimeZone is given then all the datetime fields will be according to that timezone provided.
Click https://help.alteryx.com/current/en/server/api-overview/alteryx-server-api-v3/server-api-v3-objects/schedules-endpoints.html#list-of-timezones in order to view the list of valid timezones.
Click https://help.alteryx.com/current/en/server/api-overview/alteryx-server-api-v3/server-api-v3-objects/schedules-endpoints.html#create-a-new-schedule in order to view Help Documentation for this endpoint.

parameters.name: contract
parameters.description: See Data Type Model for iteration type contract structures.

get.summary: Retrieve details about an existing subscription.
get.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: subscriptionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing subscription.

put.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: subscriptionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing subscription.

get.summary: Retrieve details about existing subscriptions.
get.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

post.summary: Create new subscription.
post.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: contract
parameters.description: The request body structure required to make a new Subscription.

delete.summary: Delete an existing subscription.
delete.description:
Only Curators can use this API endpoint.
Subscriptions can only be deleted if no workflows or users are in the studio.

parameters.name: subscriptionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing subscription.

put.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: subscriptionId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing subscription.

parameters.name: contract
parameters.description: The request body structure required to change users subscription.

200.description: Download usage records as a file.

404.description: No records are available for download.

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Upload a file containing a single ULID (EventId). All usage events with EventIds less than or equal to this ULID will be deleted.

200.description: AAC response file processed and usage events cleanup complete.

400.description: Missing or empty AAC response file.

get.summary: Retrieve all accessible user group records.
get.description:
Only Curators can use this API endpoint.
Only Custom User Groups will be retrieved. No Active Directory Groups will be returned.

post.summary: Create a new user group.
post.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

201.description: Identifier for the new User Group

get.summary: Retrieve details about an existing user group.
get.description:
Only Curators can use this API endpoint. Only works for Server user groups. 
Active Directory groups cannot be retrieved from this endpoint.

parameters.name: userGroupId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user group.

put.summary: Update name and/or role of an existing user group.
put.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: userGroupId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user group.

404.description: The response if the user group id in the url path is not found.

delete.summary: Delete an existing user group.
delete.description:
Only Curators can use this API endpoint.
Will return a 400 Bad Request if the user group is not empty and the forceDelete query param is false.

parameters.name: userGroupId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user group.

parameters.name: forceDelete
parameters.description: Delete the user group even if the group is not empty

post.summary: Add one or more existing users to an existing user group.
post.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: userGroupId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user group.

parameters.name: userIds
parameters.description: List of user ids to add to the group, separated by commas.

404.description: The reponse if the user group id in the url path is not found.

post.summary: Add an Active Directory group to an existing user group.
post.description:
Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

This endpoint can only be used for Windows Authentication configured Server instances.

parameters.name: userGroupId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user group.

parameters.name: sid
parameters.description: The identifier of the Active Directory group. This is a JSON body payload. You must submit the value wrapped in quotation marks such as: "S-My-SID".

404.description: The response if the custom user group or the active directory group are not found.

delete.summary: Remove a user from an existing user group.
delete.description:
Only Curators can use this API endpoint.
If the user is not a part of the user group, then an OK response will be returned.

parameters.name: userGroupId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user group.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user.

404.description: The response if the user group id in the url path is not found.

delete.summary: Remove an Active Directory group from an existing user group.
delete.description:
Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

This endpoint can only be used for Windows Authentication configured Server instances.

parameters.name: userGroupId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user group.

parameters.name: adGroupSid
parameters.description: Security identifer for an existing Microsoft Active Directory group.

400.description: The response if the Active Directory Group SID is not a member of the Server user group.

404.description: The response if the user group is not found.

get.summary: Retrieve details about an existing user.
get.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user.

put.summary: Update details of an existing user.
put.description:
Only Curators can use this API endpoint.
The Id of the updateContract will be overwritten by the id value in the URL.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user.

parameters.name: updateContract
parameters.description: The request body structure

delete.summary: Delete an existing user.
delete.description:
Only Curators can use this API endpoint.
If the user has any assets(workflows, schedules, collections) or exists within any user groups, then the user cannot be deleted.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user.

get.summary: Retrieve all accessible assets owned by an existing user.
get.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user.

parameters.name: assetType
parameters.description: The type of assets I want to return (defaulted to All)

get.summary: Retrieve all accessible user records.
get.description:
Only Curators can use this API endpoint.
If the searchContract.Verbose is set to false, then a reduced view object will be returned.

post.summary: Create a new user.
post.description:
Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

This endpoint cannot be used for Windows Authentication configured Server instances.

parameters.name: userContract
parameters.description: The request body structure required to make a new user

put.summary: Transfer all assets (workflows, schedules, collections) owned by one user to another.
put.description:
If any of the workflows require DCM connections, server connections, or specific run as credentials to run, these items will need to be updated before the workflow can run. 
Additionally, if users are not in the same studio, when a workflow is transferred to the new studio, all other users in the new owner's studio will also receive access to the workflow, 
and all users from the old studio will lose access

Returns a list of schedule ids that will be broken or disabled after transfer if user is deleted.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Id of the user to transfer assets from.

post.summary: Update an existing user as inactive.
post.description:
Only Curators can use this API endpoint. 
An array of user group IDs that the deactivated user is removed from.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user.

200.description: An array of user group IDs that the deactivated user is removed from.

post.summary: Send a password reset email to an existing user.
post.description:
Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

This endpoint cannot be used for Windows Authentication configured Server instances.

This endpoint cannot be used for SAML Authentication configured Server instances.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing user.

get.summary: Retrieve details about an existing workflow.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing workflow or app

put.summary: Update details of an existing workflow.
put.description:
Only Curators can use this API endpoint. 
To change the ownerId, the new owner must be in the same subscription as the current owner.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing workflow or app

delete.summary: Delete an existing workflow.
delete.description: Only Curators can use this API endpoint.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing workflow or app

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: If the workflow is scheduled, setting force to True will delete all impacted schedules before deleting the workflow.

get.summary: Download a copy of an existing workflow package.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing workflow or app

parameters.name: versionId
parameters.description: A specific version number of the workflow. If no version is provided, the published version will be downloaded.

200.description: The download stream of the yxzp.

403.description: The authenticated user does not have permission to download the workflow, or the workflow is not allowed to be downloaded directly.

get.summary: Retrieve question information for an analytic app.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing workflow or app

parameters.name: versionId
parameters.description: A specific version number of the workflow to get questions of.

403.description: The authenticated user does not have access to the workflow or questions

get.summary: Retrieve all accessible workflow records.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter results by a specific workflow name.

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description: Filter results for a specific owner.

parameters.name: createdAfter
parameters.description: Filter results based on the published revision's created date, inclusive.

parameters.name: createdBefore
parameters.description: Filter results based on the published revision's created date, inclusive.

post.summary: Upload a new workflow package.

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Must use a workflow package (.yxzp).

parameters.name: districtTags
parameters.description: Submit as JSON formatted array. IE: ["id1", "id2"]

parameters.name: sourceAppId
parameters.description:
Sets the source app id of a workflow. Can be used as the 'sourceId' reference for the POST admin/v1/workflows API endpoint.
Providing an pre existing sourceAppId will result in an invalid request.

parameters.name: executionMode
parameters.description: Accepted values are "Safe", "SemiSafe", "Standard",

parameters.name: workflowCredentialType
parameters.description: Accepted values are "Default", "Required", "Specific",

parameters.name: collectionIds
parameters.description: Submit as JSON formatted array. IE: ["id1", "id2"]

get.summary: Retrieve a list of jobs for an existing workflow.
get.description:
A Job may be returned as Completed even if the query status is Error. This would indicate an error occurred during
execution, but the workflow was run.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing workflow or app

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: The overall status of the job execution. A completed job may still have failed.

parameters.name: resultCode
parameters.description: The result code of the execution of a workflow. This can indicate a failed workflow, but a successful job.

post.summary: Creates a new job and adds it to the job execution queue.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing workflow or app

403.description: The authenticated user does not have access to execute the workflow

put.summary: Transfer specified workflow to specified owner and also schedules if desired
put.description:
If any of the workflows require DCM connections, server connections, or specific run as credentials to run, these items will need to be updated before the workflow can run. 
Additionally, if users are not in the same studio, when a workflow is transferred to the new studio, all other users in the new owner's studio will also receive access to the workflow, 
and all users from the old studio will lose access

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: Id of the workflow to transfer

post.summary: Upload a new version of an existing workflow.

parameters.name: workflowId
parameters.description: Identifier for an existing workflow or app

parameters.name: file
parameters.description: Must use a workflow package (.yxzp).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name for the updated version of the workflow.

parameters.name: ownerId
parameters.description: Identifier for the owner of the new version of the workflow.

parameters.name: makePublished
parameters.description: Updates this new version as the published version of the workflow.

parameters.name: credentialId
parameters.description: Identifier for existing credentials used within the workflow.

404.description: Indicates that the workflowId in the path is invalid

name.description: The name of the new collection.

connectionId.description: Deprecated, will (probably) be removed in the future.

DCMEObjectWrapperContract[DCMEDataSource.description:
Wrapper for supporting object linking. User can either provide a full object (to create or update it)
or just an ObjectId (to link to an existing object).

DCMEObjectWrapperContract[DCMECredential.description:
Wrapper for supporting object linking. User can either provide a full object (to create or update it)
or just an ObjectId (to link to an existing object).

JobMessageView.description: A single message of a tool in regard to a workflow execution.

text.description: Human-readable message of the job message.

toolId.description: The tool identifier within the workflow producing the message.

PagedResponse[PagedJobSummaryView].description: Represents a paginated response for public API endpoints

data.description: The data items for the current page

PagedJobSummaryView.description: Simplified job view for paged jobs API containing only essential fields

startTime.description: Time when the job started execution

queueTime.description: Time when the job was queued

totalItems.description: Total number of items across all pages

hasNextPage.description: Whether there is a next page available

hasPreviousPage.description: Whether there is a previous page available

workflowId.description: Will be null if the workflow has been deleted.

versionId.description: Will be null if the workflow has been deleted.

runDateTime.description:
RunDateTime will either be the next time the schedule will be run, OR
the next run time in the specified window of a search range.
UTC-0.

iteration.description: Contains properties for each IterationType value

timeZone.description: Optional TimeZone to be used for schedule execution.

endTime.description:
End Date Time as UTC-0
Optional, if excluded the iteration will be indefinite. 
Ignored for "Single" IterationType

hourlyContract.description: Required when the IterationType is Hourly

dailyContract.description: Required when the IterationType is Daily

weeklyContract.description: Required when the IterationType is Weekly

monthlyContract.description: Required when the IterationType is Monthly

customContract.description: Required when the IterationType is Custom

cronContract.description: Required when the IterationType is Cron Expression

dayOfMonth.description:
15 would be the 15th of each month.
Required when SimpleDayOfMonth is true.

occurrence.description:
3 would imply the 3rd X of every month.
Required when SimpleDayOfMonth is false.

dayOfWeek.description:
0 = "Sunday"
0 implies the Xth Sunday of every month.
Required when SimpleDayOfMonth is false.

daysOfMonth.description: 1, 4, 14 is the 1st, 4th, and 14th of each month.

months.description: Calendar months where January = 1 and December = 12

cronExpression.description:
Cron expressions consist of five mandatory fields. 
Each field represents a unit of time, i.e., minute, hour, day, month, etc. 
And each field in the expression is separated by a white space.
\* \* \* \* \*
\- \- \- \- \-
| | | | |
| | | | ----- Day of the week (0 - 7) (Sunday is both 0 and 7)
| | | ------- Month (1 - 12)
| | --------- Day of the month (1 - 31)
| ----------- Hour (0 - 23)
------------- Minute (0 - 59)

iteration.description: Contains properties for each IterationType value

timeZone.description:
Optional TimeZone to be used for schedule execution.

If no TimeZone is submitted, but the Iteration information changes, the system
will assume that the Schedule's existing time zone is to be retained.

workflowId.description:
The application id for the workflow in the gallery DB.

Will be null if the workflow has been deleted.

runDateTime.description:
RunDateTime will either be the next time the schedule will be run, OR
the next run time in the specified window of a search range

iteration.description: Contains a property corresponding to each IterationType value.

priority.description:
Optional Priority of schedule execution. 
Authenticated user must be enabled to set priority or be a Curator, else this property is ignored.

workerTag.description:
Optional WorkerTag for schedule execution.
Authenticated user must be enabled to set worker tags, else this property is ignored.

credentialId.description: Optional Credential to be used for schedule execution.

timeZone.description: Optional TimeZone to be used for schedule execution.

InvalidRequestResponseBody.description: SwaggerResponse-friendly object representing the response body structure of a BadRequest's ModelState

id.description: The id of the user to be updated. Optional as will be set via the controller path parameter.

firstName.description: This field is required on non windows authentication type.

lastName.description: This field is required on non windows authentication type.

ReducedUserView.description: A subset of UserView properties to be returned for smaller payloads

role.description:
If no role is provided, the user will be set as Evaluated.

Accepted values are "NoAccess", "Viewer", "Member", "Artisan", "Curator", "Evaluated"

WorkflowJobView.description: Represents limited information about a workflow's job
